geographic_scope: Subnational; initial fighting across North, West, and South Darfur with concentration in North Darfur and Jebel Marra corridors
theater_description: Early rebel offensives, including the El Fasher airport attack, and the transition from localized rebellion to region-wide counterinsurgency.
geometry_method_note: Would need early 2003 conflict-site reconstruction rather than later DHP affected-population polygons; no full denominator built here.
military_area_note: Regular SAF, Janjaweed mobilization sites, and early SLA/JEM operating zones.
civilian_area_note: Rural villages and market towns affected by first-wave village attacks and displacement; no stable monthly affected-population series recovered for this onset phase.
split_reason: Data-poor onset phase with different belligerent balance and no robust monthly civilian exposure series; CRED excluded Period 1 from direct estimation.
quality_note: High historical importance but sparse clean numerator/denominator separation.

geographic_scope: Subnational Darfur-wide affected-population footprint across North, South, and West Darfur
theater_description: Government-Janjaweed counter-offensives and widespread village destruction across Darfur, with the heaviest direct violent mortality and rapidly accelerating displacement.
geometry_method_note: Civilian footprint approximated from Darfur Humanitarian Profile affected-population counts as used by CRED, excluding the three state capitals and focusing on IDPs plus affected residents in assessed sites.
military_area_note: Includes SAF, Janjaweed, and Darfur rebel forces active in Dar Zaghawa, Jebel Marra, West Darfur rural belts, and adjacent staging areas tied directly to the Darfur theater.
civilian_area_note: IDP camps/towns hosting IDPs and conflict-affected resident settlements in the three Darfur states; not the whole Darfur population.
split_reason: Highest violence, distinctive counterinsurgency mode, weak humanitarian access, and best direct-violent civilian reconstruction from later retrospective work.
quality_note: Civilian violent mortality is reasonably triangulated, but combatant mortality remains model-generated and broad.

geographic_scope: Subnational Darfur-wide affected-population footprint across North, South, and West Darfur
theater_description: Violence remained high but humanitarian access expanded sharply, IDP settlement geography stabilized somewhat, and affected-population reporting improved.
geometry_method_note: Civilian footprint approximated from DHP/OCHA affected-population snapshots and CRED’s Darfur-wide application of those data; still excludes the three state capitals.
military_area_note: Same umbrella of SAF, Janjaweed, and rebels, but more spatially fragmented after the April humanitarian ceasefire.
civilian_area_note: Conflict-affected residents plus IDPs in assessed Darfur localities, not all six million regional residents.
split_reason: Different violence mode and much better denominator documentation after April 2004 humanitarian scale-up.
quality_note: Civilian mortality and civilian exposure are better documented than combatant exposure; DR remains highly uncertain.

geographic_scope: Subnational Darfur-wide, increasingly fragmented by camp systems and localized fronts
theater_description: Violence declines from the 2003-04 peak but remains serious; disease dominates mixed mortality while fighting persists.
geometry_method_note: Would require monthly DHP reconstruction and actor-specific fronts.
military_area_note: Fragmenting rebel and militia formations complicate combatant denominator assignment.
civilian_area_note: Large camp and host-community populations with shifting exposure and aid access.
split_reason: Different mortality regime; direct violence lower relative to indirect mortality and pre-DPA actor structure still distinct from later fragmentation.
quality_note: Civilian mixed mortality better known than clean direct civilian vs combatant separation.

geographic_scope: Subnational, multi-front Darfur with rising fragmentation
theater_description: After the Darfur Peace Agreement, armed actors splintered, fronts multiplied, and localized militia/rebel violence changed the exposure map.
geometry_method_note: Needs district-level front reconstruction and separate treatment of camp violence vs rural fighting.
military_area_note: Many splinter factions with uncertain force sizes and overlapping alliances.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure becomes highly nonuniform across camps, roads, and rural pockets.
split_reason: Belligerent structure changed materially after the DPA.
quality_note: Episodeization is clear, but core DR inputs are not yet recoverable with confidence.

geographic_scope: Subnational Darfur with peacekeeping overlay and fragmented fronts
theater_description: Continued fragmented conflict under the UNAMID period, with direct violence lower than in 2003-04 but still recurrent.
geometry_method_note: Would need event-level mapping and peacekeeping-presence-adjusted exposure.
military_area_note: Combatant classification complicated by splinters, auxiliaries, and local defense groups.
split_reason: Peacekeeping era and fragmented violence differ from both pre-DPA and later inter-Arab/localized conflict.
quality_note: Usable civilian direct-death signal exists, but theater-specific combatant exposure remains sparse.

geographic_scope: Subnational, localized fronts and inter-communal violence across Darfur
theater_description: Lower-intensity but persistent conflict increasingly entangled with local Arab-Arab and tribal violence.
geometry_method_note: Needs separate coding of insurgent, state, and communal micro-theaters.
military_area_note: Combatant and militia roles overlap with communal violence.
civilian_area_note: Exposure concentrated in discrete districts and camp corridors rather than a single Darfur-wide footprint.
split_reason: Violence mode and actor structure changed materially.
quality_note: Whole-episode DR would be misleading without micro-episode splitting.

geographic_scope: Subnational, especially Jebel Marra and adjacent Darfur districts
theater_description: Renewed major operations, especially around Jebel Marra, with allegations of severe attacks on civilians.
geometry_method_note: Requires mountain-corridor and displacement-route geometry rather than Darfur-wide averages.
military_area_note: Mountain warfare and factional fronts complicate denominator reconstruction.
civilian_area_note: Exposure likely concentrated in Jebel Marra highlands and adjacent settlements.
split_reason: Material geographic refocusing on Jebel Marra and renewed offensive operations.
quality_note: Potentially estimable with dedicated theater clipping, but not from the broad-source pass completed here.

theater_description: Residual violence after the main insurgent phases, before the 2023 Sudan war reopened the Darfur theater at scale.
geometry_method_note: Would require localized district episodes rather than a single residual phase.
military_area_note: Militia, local-defense, and state-affiliated forces blend.
civilian_area_note: Patchy local exposure; Darfur-wide denominator would overstate exposure.
split_reason: Different intensity regime and pre-2023 lull before a new war family stage.
quality_note: Too heterogeneous for one denominator without more sub-splitting.

episode_name: Darfur theater of the Sudan civil war
geographic_scope: Subnational Darfur with city sieges, camp massacres, and cross-border displacement
theater_description: RSF-SAF war in Darfur, including the al-Fashir siege, camp attacks, and ethnically targeted violence in West and North Darfur.
geometry_method_note: Needs city/camp-level polygons (e.g., al-Fashir, Zamzam, Abu Shouk, El Geneina corridors) and separate monthly force-presence mapping.
military_area_note: RSF, SAF, allied militias, and camp- or city-based army-aligned groups require theater-specific force reconstruction.
civilian_area_note: Massively displaced populations concentrated in camps, besieged cities, and flight corridors to Chad.
split_reason: New war family stage beginning 15 April 2023 with different belligerents, urban siege dynamics, and new atrocity pattern.
quality_note: Civilian harm is well documented, but strict combatant/civilian separation and person-month combatant exposure remain too weak for a responsible DR here.
